title: Standard library Map rises abort
body:
Hello, 

I am working with lowrisc-chip fork. I have been using verilator with this model for years. But now I am experiencing a weird bug. When I change my verilator version from 4.030 to 4.040 or above (I have tried with all of them) I am experiencing a weird abort when initializing some structures. The function that rises the abort is **memory_model_init** at [dpi_ram_behav](https://github.com/lowRISC/lowrisc-chip/blob/refresh-v0.6/src/test/cxx/common/dpi_ram_behav.cpp). 

I am using gcc and g++ version 10, but it also happens with version 9.

I have used GDB to see the back trace, and what I get is: 

at 0x4C2318B: raise (raise.c:51)
by 0x4C02858: abort (abort.c:79)
by 0x4BA0675: ??? (in /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgcc_s.so.1)
by 0x11146E: memory_model_init (dpi_ram_behav.cpp:131)

Specifically I see that the destructor of a map rises the abort:
/usr/include/c++/10/bits/stl_map.h

```
#if __cplusplus >= 201103L
      /**
       *  The dtor only erases the elements, and note that if the elements
       *  themselves are pointers, the pointed-to memory is not touched in any
       *  way.  Managing the pointer is the user's responsibility.
       */
      ~map() = default;
#endif
```

At first I thought that maybe the issue was related with thread safe functions, but after trying different thread flags (no-threads, threads 1, etc),  I was not able to avoid the error. I also thought that maybe c++11 support was the problem, but I was using std=c++11 with verilator v4.030
